data.overview: UK M&E drawings use symbols defined by BS 1192, BS EN ISO 7519, and CIBSE conventions. However, EVERY project may define its own symbols in the legend. The legend ALWAYS overrides standard symbols. If no legend exists, fall back to these standards but flag all identifications as [NO LEGEND — ASSUMED FROM STANDARD].

line_types.solid_line: Pipework at the level shown (visible on this floor)
line_types.dashed_line: Pipework above or below the level shown (typically in ceiling void or below floor)

flow_direction.arrow_on_line: Indicates flow direction — critical for distinguishing flow from return
flow_direction.colours_if_used: Red = LTHW flow, Blue = CHW or CWS, Green = LTHW return (varies by project)

valves.gate_valve: Two triangles meeting at points (bowtie shape)
valves.ball_valve: Circle on the pipe line with a line through it
valves.butterfly_valve: Triangle or diamond on the pipe line
valves.check_valve: Triangle pointing in flow direction with a bar
valves.pressure_relief: Triangle with arrow pointing away from pipe
valves.control_valve: Valve symbol with 'CV' or actuator symbol (M for motorised)
valves.strainer: Y-shape or diamond with mesh pattern
valves.picv: Pressure-independent control valve — 'PICV' label or specific symbol
valves.warning: Valve symbols vary SIGNIFICANTLY between projects. Never assume — check legend.

equipment.pump: Circle with arrow or impeller symbol. Label: 'P-01', 'CHP-01', etc.
equipment.expansion_vessel: Circle or oval, often with 'EV' label
equipment.boiler: Rectangle with flame symbol or 'B-01' label
equipment.heat_exchanger: Rectangle with diagonal lines or 'PHE-01' label

vertical_movement.rise_symbol: Circle with 'R' — pipe rises through floor above
vertical_movement.drop_symbol: Circle with 'D' — pipe drops through floor below
vertical_movement.riser_reference: Usually labelled with riser number: 'R1', 'R2', etc.

line_types.solid_outline: Ductwork at the level shown — drawn as two parallel lines (rectangular) or single line (circular)
line_types.rectangular: Two parallel lines with dimension annotation (e.g., 600×400)
line_types.circular: Single line with diameter annotation (e.g., Ø315)
line_types.dashed_outline: Ductwork on another level or hidden behind other services

dampers.fire_damper: Thick bar or 'FD' symbol at duct/wall intersection. ALWAYS count these — life safety item.
dampers.volume_control: Butterfly symbol on duct line — 'VCD' label. Motorised has 'M' symbol.
dampers.non_return: 'NRD' or 'BD' — backdraught damper at fan discharge or external openings

terminals.linear_diffuser: Long thin rectangle along ceiling edge
terminals.return_grille: Square with X pattern (crossed diagonals) or parallel bars
terminals.extract_grille: Similar to return — check label for system type
terminals.transfer_grille: Grille symbol in a wall or door — NOT connected to ductwork

wiring_accessories.data_outlet: Triangle or 'D' symbol (varies greatly by project)

lighting.note: Luminaire symbols vary WILDLY between projects. ALWAYS check legend.
lighting.emergency: Usually has 'EM' suffix or different fill
lighting.external: Circle or rectangle with different fill

fire_alarm.sounder: Circle or triangle with 'S' or bell symbol

containment.cable_tray: Dashed line with 'CT' label and width annotation
containment.conduit: Thin line with 'C' label or diameter notation

fire_stopping.fire_rated_wall: Thick line or cross-hatched wall with fire rating annotation (e.g., '60/60')
fire_stopping.fire_collar: Ring symbol at pipe/duct penetration through rated element

fire_damper_identification.rule: Fire dampers MUST be at every ductwork penetration through a fire-rated wall or floor. If not shown on drawings but fire compartmentation exists, flag as [FIRE DAMPERS POTENTIALLY MISSING].
drawing_clues: Thick bar across duct at wall line
Red or orange highlighting at penetrations

legend_reading_process.rule: ALWAYS read and index the legend before measuring. The legend defines every symbol for THIS project.
process: 1. Locate the legend/key — usually on the first sheet, in the title block area, or on a separate legend sheet.
2. Index every symbol: note the symbol graphic, its description, and any size/specification information.
3. For each symbol, determine: is it an item to COUNT (nr) or a route to MEASURE (m)?
4. Where the legend is incomplete or missing, note which symbols are unidentified.
5. For unidentified symbols: describe the shape/appearance, note its location on the drawing, flag as [UNIDENTIFIED SYMBOL — LEGEND REQUIRED].
6. DO NOT guess what an unidentified symbol represents. A wrong identification is worse than flagging it as unknown.
